source: Campaign countdown clocks — update filled/ticks manually after each session. See context/post-session-runbook.md step 2.10.

clocks.label: Case C — Nadia's Window
clocks.description: Nadia Osei's consciousness is tethered to information infrastructure. The tether frays with time. When it goes, she disperses — and the MESA pattern she assembled goes with her.
clocks.advancement_note: Advance 1 tick per session in which hunters make no contact with Nadia. At 4/4: Nadia disperses, MESA pattern is lost, Case C closes without resolution.
clocks.notes: CAMPBELL timed this briefing deliberately. If hunters debrief Nadia fully, Victor learns things CAMPBELL chose not to report.

clocks.label: Case D — Mira Okonkwo
clocks.description: Martin Wentworth's obligation mechanism is building toward a specific act. His most recent letter names a target: Mira Okonkwo, 9 years old.
clocks.advancement_note: Advance 1 tick per session of no hunter engagement. Advance 2 ticks if MESA closes the estate archive before hunters access it. At 6/6: Mira dies; Case D closes as a failure.
clocks.notes: Wentworth communicates only through formal letters. The estate archive — which MESA likely secured — may contain the method for resolving the mechanism.

clocks.label: Case E — Saturday's Performance
clocks.description: The remaining four understudies are rehearsing. The performance space has a thin-boundary resonance — the same mechanism that took the first three. After Saturday's show, four more identities may be lost.
segment_labels: Opening night approaches — dress rehearsal complete
Day before — last chance to intervene before the performance
clocks.advancement_note: Advance 1 tick when Saturday approaches in the fiction. At 4/4: four understudies lose their identities; Case E closes as a failure. Sven is the natural resolution vector — prioritise if clock reaches 3.
clocks.notes: Grant source: Solstice Property Group (MESA shell company). The mechanism requires a theatrical farewell within the logic of performance. Clara Voss and Edmund Farrow's deaths are the anchoring event.

clocks.label: MESA — Response to PORTAL
clocks.description: MESA detected PORTAL's presence at the Aldermoor estate office. The internal threat reclassification was approved. They are profiling PORTAL's field team and modelling future responses.
clocks.advancement_note: Advance 1 tick when: hunters make further contact with MESA assets, a MESA operative identifies a hunter by name, or Dan Nilsson's modelling produces actionable intelligence. At 4/4: MESA moves directly against PORTAL — Rook arrives with a specific objective.
clocks.notes: Rook's reclassification approved post-S02. Dan Nilsson is actively modelling PORTAL's field responses. Substance Θ, Anchor Spikes, and BIM Collection Arrays may be deployed against the team.

clocks.label: Aldermoor — Cartographer Stability
clocks.description: The Cartographer retracted to its pre-expansion boundary after the field team's intervention. The excavation site is sealed but not filled. If MESA's BIM collection disturbs the substrate, the boundary fails.
segment_labels: BIM collection breaches excavation seal — expansion resumes
Full re-expansion — Aldermoor zone active again
clocks.advancement_note: Advance 1 tick if MESA's collection activity intensifies or the excavation site is physically disturbed. Advance 2 ticks if MESA intentionally breaches the seal. Do not advance if PORTAL intervenes to halt collection.
clocks.notes: Background clock. Not urgent unless MESA escalates. The Cartographer is quiet — it knows the team. A direct attempt to communicate (especially from Sven or Rex) could stabilise the clock permanently.
